description_long: This structure is a multi-level transformer tower with a robust, industrial design, featuring a steel frame and multiple horizontal crossarms. The tower is built on a green base, likely simulating grass or ground, and has a flat, corrugated metal roof with two prominent support brackets on either side. The sides of the tower are enclosed with metal grilles and panels, while the front and rear reveal staircases that provide access to the upper levels. The central section is open, exposing the internal stairs and some electrical components, with the crossarms extending outward to support power lines. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on functionality and durability, suitable for urban or industrial environments.
description: A multi-level, steel-framed transformer tower with crossarms, metal grilles, and exposed staircases, set on a green base. The structure is industrial and robust, designed for supporting electrical equipment and power lines.
description_view_0: The side view shows the metal grilles and panels, with the support brackets on the roof clearly visible.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the open central section with visible staircases and the layered crossarms extending outward.
description_view_2: The opposite side view mirrors the first, with metal grilles and panels, but less visibility of the internal stairs.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the staircase more prominently, with an open section and less crossarm visibility.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: There are two main horizontal crossarms on the upper level, parallel to each other and extending outward from both sides of the structure.
attribute_configuration_of_attached_powe: No power lines are visibly attached; the crossarms are present but do not have any wires or cables connected.
